description: A moisture-limited contrast case where thermals rise but shallow cumulus fails to form meaningfully.
physical_question: How does insufficient low-level moisture prevent shallow cumulus formation even when boundary-layer thermals and vertical motion are present?
learning_goals: Compare w-without-qc behavior against the validated baseline.
Identify low-level moisture and saturation deficit as the main limiting factor.
Separate a healthy failed-cloud case from a dead or numerically broken no-cloud run.
intended_behavior: A drier contrast around the external-sounding Baseline Shallow Cumulus setup.
expected_behavior: Boundary-layer thermals and vertical motion remain, but cloud water stays below the cloud-formation diagnostic threshold or appears only as trace amounts.

controls.label: Low-level humidity
controls.description: Lower-atmosphere moisture relative to the baseline.
controls.cm1_mapping_notes: Maps to a drier external input_sounding profile while preserving the validated baseline dynamics.

controls.label: Surface heating
controls.description: Heating strength available to grow thermals.
controls.cm1_mapping_notes: Held at the validated baseline for the first Dry Failed implementation.

run_size_presets.label: Quick look
run_size_presets.purpose: Check whether dry setup suppresses cloud formation.

expected_diagnostics.cloud_water_summary: summarize near-zero or weak cloud-water signal
notes: Failed cloud formation is useful only when vertical motion remains healthy and target fields avoid severe NaN/Infinity caveats.

cm1_template.mapping_notes: Derived from the accepted external-sounding Baseline Shallow Cumulus path by drying the lower-atmosphere moisture profile.

warnings: No cloud plus no vertical motion is not an accepted Dry Failed result.
limitations: Dry failure behavior requires local/manual CM1 validation.
The first implementation intentionally varies only low-level moisture.
